title: correa reflexa var. nummulariifolia
intro: Correa reflexa var. nummulariifolia, also known as the coin-leaf correa, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant native to Australia. It is a shrub with distinctive coin-shaped leaves and fragrant yellow flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its use in treating health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Correa reflexa var. nummulariifolia are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
